---
title: "Practice: Effective And Honest Scales"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
```

# What We Practiced

The module showed how to:

- compare full and focused axes
- use percent scales carefully
- decide when zero is needed
- make scale choices visible to readers

# Task

Pick one dataset and create:

1. a first plot
2. a revised version with a different scale choice
3. a short interpretation of the tradeoff

Suggested datasets:

- `data/real/flu_vaccination_age_time_clean.csv`
- `data/real/state_population_ranks.csv`
- `data/simulated/respiratory_booster_coverage.csv`

Helpful module reference: `modules/09_communication-polish/01_effective-and-honest-scales.qmd`.

# Work

```{r}
# Load data, build a first plot, then build a revised scale version.
```

# Interpretation

Write briefly: which scale choice is more honest for the comparison and what tradeoff it makes.
